item.title: Pfizer Cancer Campaign and Pipeline
item.description:
• Summary: Pfizer has launched a new cancer awareness campaign featuring former professional baseball player Fernando Mendoza as a face of its 'Game of Inches' initiative, aiming to educate patients and caregivers about treatment options. Simultaneously, the company has culled an early-stage PD-L1 program after a series of clinical wins and deals in cancer, indicating a strategic pivot toward more promising assets in its oncology pipeline.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: Fierce Pharma, BioSpace

item.title: Novartis Pluvicto EU Filing Withdrawal
item.description:
• Summary: Novartis has withdrawn its application in the European Union for a label expansion of Pluvicto (lutetium Lu-177 vipivotide tetraxetan) in prostate cancer. The withdrawal comes as the company reassesses its regulatory strategy. Pluvicto is a radioligand therapy targeting PSMA-expressing tumors and is already approved for certain patients with metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer. The EU decision does not affect existing approvals.
  • MoAs: Lutetium-177-PSMA-617 radioligand targeting PSMA
  • Sources: Endpoints News

item.title: Merck PD-1/VEGF Data and Zai Lab
item.description:
• Summary: Merck has released new clinical data on a combination therapy involving its PD-1 inhibitor pembrolizumab (Keytruda) and a VEGF inhibitor, with Zai Lab as a development partner. The data were presented at a medical meeting, highlighting efficacy in certain tumor types. The collaboration underscores the growing trend of PD-1/VEGF combinations in oncology, and the article also notes a record IPO for Kailera, though details on that are limited.
  • MoAs: PD-1 checkpoint inhibitor: blocks PD-1 receptor on T cells, enhancing anti-tumor immune response; VEGF inhibitor: blocks vascular endothelial growth factor, inhibiting angiogenesis and tumor growth.
  • Sources: Fierce Pharma
